name: Ronnell Bright
abstract: Ronnell Lovelace Bright (July 3, 1930 – August 12, 2021) was an American jazz pianist. He made cameo appearances in the TV shows The Jeffersons and Sanford and Son, also working on The Carol Burnett Show.

concerts.label: Jack Humphrey Septet/ Maynard Ferguson and His Orchestra/ Sarah Vaughan/ Modern Jazz Quartet

works.t: Jubilee: Just One of Those Things

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Jack Humphrey Septet/ Maynard Ferguson and His Orchestra/ Sarah Vaughan/ Modern Jazz Quartet

works.t: Jubilee: Just One of Those Things

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Ronnell Bright was on the date... We had a lot of fine players... I think they used Thurman Green, Ronnell Bright and me.

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Haynes
about_quotes.evidence: Then when he left, I think Ronnell Bright. ... Then when Ronnell Bright came in, it started changing
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy Haynes — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: Floyd Morris, Ronnell Bright who was Nancy Wilson’s accompanist. All the piano players that played.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: The guys have been with me for so long... The main ones were Don Trenner, Ronnell, and Mike Wolff.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: Ronnell Bright (I think he was working with her), to come and fill in. So I go play with her.
